stages.name: unit_tests
stages.evidence: test/runner_baseline_test 'minimalEnviron restricts the command environment to the documented allowlist' asserts runner.minimalEnviron copies only the allowlisted parent keys (PATH/HOME present), forces LC_ALL=C and LANG=C (not the inherited en_US.UTF-8), omits absent keys (TMPDIR/ZIG_*), and drops a non-allowlisted secret (ZNTL_SECRET) -- so the executor truly restricts the env and environment_policy=minimal is truthful. Reverting minimalEnviron to inherit-all made the test fail (LC_ALL not forced, secret leaked). The real-binary integration test (task 111) also passed with the restricted env, proving `zig test` still works under the minimal allowlist. Full suite green (501 tests).

stages.name: mutation_checks
stages.reason: task 112 makes the reported environment policy truthful by implementing the documented minimal allowlist; the fix is proven by a unit test that fails (env not restricted/normalized) under the pre-fix inherit-all behavior, plus the real-binary integration test passing under the restricted env. Full self-mutation dogfood is gated to task 085.

residual_risk: This task was briefly blocked (scope_gap): the only portable Zig-0.16 source of the parent environment is std.process.Init.environ_map, which is reachable only by threading it through src/main.zig -> cli.run; the user authorized adding src/main.zig (and this task file) to allowed_files to take the implement path. The alternative relabel path would have needed schemas/report.v1.schema.json and six snapshots, all out of scope.

The minimal allowlist is exactly PATH/HOME/TMPDIR/ZIG_GLOBAL_CACHE_DIR/ZIG_LOCAL_CACHE_DIR plus forced LC_ALL=C/LANG=C. `zig test` works under it on the dev/CI toolchain (verified by the task-111 integration test); a host whose toolchain needs an additional variable (e.g. a non-default macOS SDK pinned via SDKROOT, or a custom linker via a non-PATH var) would need that key added to runner.env_allowlist and docs/SANDBOX_SECURITY.md. zentinel omits absent allowlisted keys rather than synthesizing them.

Only the run command's test-command executors (baseline + mutant, via RunCtx) are restricted; the version/check `zig version` discovery and the doctest runner spawn separately and are not part of the mutation report's command records, so their environment_policy is not claimed there. report.zig and mutant_runner.zig were in allowed_files but needed no change: the label stays `minimal` and is now accurate.
